title: Morning Robotics Scan
report:
## Isaac Sim-to-Real Quadruped

### The Breakthrough
A reinforcement‑learning framework trained in Nvidia Isaac Sim transfers zero‑shot to a real Unitree Go1, matching the built‑in controller on velocity tracking while handling larger disturbances and reaching 2.0 m/s linear and 1.8 rad/s angular speeds.

### The Caveats
The work evaluates only a single Go1 robot and offers no comparison to existing RL or model‑based locomotion baselines.

## NICO Sim-to-Real Grasping

### The Breakthrough
The pipeline combines YOLO hand/object detection, stereo fisheye vision, a nonlinear calibration model and a visual‑feedback loop, eliminating external trackers and achieving the highest overall tabletop grasp success across the workspace.

### The Caveats
Gains are reported only against the authors' own baselines; no comparison to existing sim‑to‑real methods or other robots is provided.

## RoboHarness Policy Orchestration

### The Breakthrough
RoboHarness builds a multi‑modal execution memory and a Memory Bridge that retrieves prior trajectories and guides handoffs between heterogeneous controllers, delivering substantial zero‑shot long‑horizon planning gains and out‑of‑distribution robustness on benchmarks and real‑robot trials.

### The Caveats
The paper supplies no named baseline comparisons, making the magnitude of improvement unclear.

## FM-VLA Force Memory for Manipulation

### The Breakthrough
FM‑VLA encodes force time‑series into low‑dimensional latent tokens via a VAE and conditions a vision‑language‑action model, achieving >80 % success on three memory‑dependent contact‑rich tasks with minimal inference overhead.

### The Caveats
No baseline is presented, so the reported improvement is unsubstantiated.

## daft-physical-ai Data Library

### The Breakthrough
The library offers Daft UDFs for lazy hand‑tracking (MediaPipe or WiLoR) and reward scoring with a 4 B‑parameter Robometer model, reading LeRobot datasets up to 15× faster and reporting MediaPipe detection = 100 % and PCK@0.1/0.2/0.3 = 49/84/96 on EgoDex.

### The Caveats
Speed and accuracy gains are unverified against existing preprocessing frameworks.

## Agent Swarms for SQLite Build

### The Breakthrough
A planner/worker tree‑decomposed swarm uses strong planner agents to generate sub‑goals and cheap worker agents to execute them, reaching 80 % of a held‑out SQLite test suite in four hours with ~1,000 commits/s, outperforming the prior version by severalfold.

### The Caveats
Validation is limited to a single SQLite build; broader applicability is not demonstrated.

## AndroidEnv RL Library

### The Breakthrough
AndroidEnv wraps a real‑time Android emulator as an RL environment with a hybrid action space (continuous touch coordinates plus discrete touch/lift) and high‑resolution RGB observations that preserve exact UI dynamics.

### The Caveats
The repository provides no RL algorithm benchmarks or hardware tests to confirm practical usefulness.

## ExecuTorch Edge Runtime

### The Breakthrough
ExecuTorch AOT‑compiles PyTorch models into a portable .pte file, supporting 12+ hardware back‑ends with a 50 KB base footprint, enabling one‑click switching between CPU, GPU, NPU, and custom accelerators for on‑device AI.

### The Caveats
Hardware‑specific speedups and deployment claims lack benchmarks or comparisons to existing runtimes.

## RoSys All-Python Robot Framework

### The Breakthrough
RoSys replaces ROS middleware with an asyncio‑driven pure‑Python architecture, coupling NiceGUI for a web UI and providing a simulation layer with time‑acceleration, aiming for lock‑free parallelism and easy offloading of CPU‑heavy tasks.

### The Caveats
No performance benchmarks, real‑world robot tests, or ROS comparisons are provided.

### Priority
The Isaac Sim-to-Real quadruped result is the most immediate hardware breakthrough, showing zero‑shot transfer of a high‑performance locomotion policy to a real robot.

### One action
Download the released policy and try reproducing the zero‑shot transfer on your own quadruped platform.

### What to watch
Several papers this week push sim‑to‑real pipelines—Isaac’s quadruped locomotion, NICO’s calibrated grasping, and RoboHarness’s memory‑driven policy orchestration—watch for follow‑up benchmarks and broader robot evaluations.

Optimizing sim-to-real grasping on the low-cost humanoid robot NICO
RoboHarness: Memory-Driven Orchestration of Heterogeneous Robot Policies for Long-Horizon Planning
FM-VLA: Force-based Memory Boosts Vision-Language-Action for Contact-Rich Tasks
daft-physical-ai: Python library to turn robot video into training‑ready data
Agent swarms hit 80% SQLite build success using planner/worker tree decomposition
AndroidEnv: Python library turning Android devices into RL environments
ExecuTorch: PyTorch’s unified on‑device AI runtime for mobile, embedded and edge
RoSys: All‑Python robot system with web UI and asyncio
